name: Spicy Piloncillo Syrup
recipeInstructions:
Note: Piloncillo is an unrefined sugar that comes in a firm cone shape (it's named after the pilon, a conical weight used on scales).
Piloncillo is available at Hispanic supermarkets.
Place water, sugar and spices in heavy one-quart nonreactive saucepan.
Let sit for 1 hour or longer, until sugar is fully dissolved.
Slowly bring to a boil over medium heat, then reduce heat and simmer for 4-5 minutes.
Allow to cool completely, then discard the spices. Pour the remaining mixture into a glass bottle.
This dark syrup dissolves easily in hot coffee drinks.
Nutritional analysis per tablespoon: 137 calories, trace of fat, 35 grams carbohydrates, 0 cholesterol, 6 milligrams sodium.
Source: Tequila: Cooking with the Spirit of Mexico
Fort Worth Star Telegram -- Food section -- 29 November 1995
